bibliographic_data.title: RANSAC Back to SOTA: A Two-stage Consensus Filtering for Real-time 3D Registration
bibliographic_data.abstract: Correspondence-based point cloud registration (PCR) plays a key role in robotics and computer vision. However, challenges like sensor noises, object occlusions, and descriptor limitations inevitably result in numerous outliers. RANSAC family is the most popular outlier removal solution. However, the requisite iterations escalate exponentially with the outlier ratio, rendering it far inferior to existing methods (SC2PCR [1], MAC [2], etc.) in terms of accuracy or speed. Thus, we propose a two-stage consensus filtering (TCF) that elevates RANSAC to state-of-the-art (SOTA) speed and accuracy. Firstly, one-point RANSAC obtains a consensus set based on length consistency. Subsequently, two-point RANSAC refines the set via angle consistency. Then, three-point RANSAC computes a coarse pose and removes outliers based on transformed correspondence’s distances. Drawing on optimizations from one-point and two-point RANSAC, three-point RANSAC requires only a few iterations. Eventually, an iterative reweighted least squares (IRLS) is applied to yield the optimal pose. Experiments on the large-scale KITTI and ETH datasets demonstrate our method achieves up to three-orders-of-magnitude speedup compared to MAC while maintaining registration accuracy and recall.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes a two-stage consensus filtering (TCF) method that significantly boosts the speed of RANSAC while maintaining accuracy in real-time 3D registration.
core_content.key_methodology: The TCF method consists of three stages: one-point RANSAC for length consistency, two-point RANSAC for angle consistency, and three-point RANSAC for computing a coarse pose and removing outliers. An iterative reweighted least squares (IRLS) is then applied to refine the final pose.
outcomes: Achieves up to three-orders-of-magnitude speedup compared to MAC
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper addresses the challenge of correspondence-based point cloud registration (PCR) in robotics, where sensor noises, object occlusions, and descriptor limitations lead to numerous outliers. The authors propose a two-stage consensus filtering (TCF) method that significantly boosts the speed of RANSAC while maintaining accuracy. The TCF method consists of three stages: one-point RANSAC for length consistency, two-point RANSAC for angle consistency, and three-point RANSAC for computing a coarse pose and removing outliers. An iterative reweighted least squares (IRLS) is then applied to refine the final pose. Experiments on large-scale KITTI and ETH datasets demonstrate that their method achieves up to three-orders-of-magnitude speedup compared to MAC while maintaining registration accuracy and recall.
